title: Retry Safety, Replayability, Backoff, Jitter, and Retry Budgets · Browser and Node Client Systems
description: A high-traffic web SDK retries selected reads under 429 and 503 without replaying one-shot streams or synchronizing every browser tab. Learners cumulatively produce a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded retry and replay decision engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify retry candidates from operation semantics, failure phase, response status, caller budget, and server guidance. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Prove request bodies and credential state can be reconstructed before replaying an attempt. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Apply capped exponential backoff with an explicit jitter strategy and deterministic test clock. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
